name: Pelé
bio: The only player to win three FIFA World Cups, Pelé is the most decorated footballer in history. Born in poverty in Três Corações, he became football's first global superstar — a symbol of Brazilian joy and an ambassador for the sport until his death in 2022.

name: Diego Maradona
bio: From the slums of Villa Fiorito to the pinnacle of world football, Maradona remains the sport's most mythologised figure. His 1986 World Cup — arguably the greatest individual tournament in history — cemented a legacy of genius inseparable from controversy.

name: Johan Cruyff
bio: More than any other footballer, Johan Cruyff changed how the game is played. Total Football, the 4-3-3, positional fluidity, pressing — all trace back to Cruyff. As a player and manager at Barcelona, he shaped a footballing philosophy still dominant today.

name: Franz Beckenbauer
bio: The Kaiser invented a position. Beckenbauer redefined the role of the defender — sweeping forward, initiating attacks, reading the game like a conductor. He won the World Cup as both captain and manager, a feat matched by no one else.

name: Zinedine Zidane
bio: Grace under pressure. Zidane's genius was his ability to impose absolute calm in the biggest moments — his two headers in the 1998 World Cup final, his Champions League volley in 2002. Off the pitch, his three consecutive Champions League titles as manager are unprecedented.

name: Ronaldinho
bio: Football's greatest entertainer. At the peak of his powers at Barcelona (2004–2006), Ronaldinho played a form of football that made opponents applaud mid-match. His influence on Messi — his teammate and protégé — may be his most lasting contribution to the sport.

name: Ronaldo Nazário
bio: The original Phenomenon. Before injuries stole his best years, Ronaldo was considered the most complete striker the world had ever seen — speed, skill, finishing, strength. His redemption arc at the 2002 World Cup, scoring both goals in the final, is one of sport's great stories.

name: Lionel Messi
bio: Eight Ballon d'Or awards. The 2022 World Cup. The man who finally ended Argentina's 36-year international drought and did it the way only he could — with late goals, penalties, and a final performance that settled the debate for millions. The most decorated player in football history.

name: Cristiano Ronaldo
bio: The ultimate self-made athlete. Ronaldo arrived at Manchester United as a showboating teenager and forged himself into the most physically dominant forward of his era through sheer will. Five Champions Leagues, five Ballon d'Ors, and the all-time international scoring record.

name: Andrés Iniesta
bio: The quiet genius. Iniesta's goal in the 2010 World Cup final — scored during one of the most difficult periods of his personal life — is one of football's most emotional moments. His partnership with Xavi at Barcelona was the engine of the most successful club and national team of the 2000s.
